content: Has anyone worked on changing our static mut uses to use an UnsafeCell wrapper? Benno mentioned that @Alice Ryhl you may be working on this
I have an incomplete patch somewhere that changes the one in the module macro.
I did see you discuss it in the module params thread. I was going to reply after the weekend.
Yeah, that's where it came up because there it would introduce more static mut
So I was thinking we should add an UnsafeSyncCell that can be used by macros

My incomplete change just adds a new struct for the specific case, but sure. It's useful enough that std has an unstable implementation of exactly that: https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/std/cell/struct.SyncUnsafeCell.html
That one doesn't work because it only adds Sync if T: Sync
Which is my biggest gripe with it
and bindings::my_random_struct has raw pointers .. got it
Are you okay with me putting something new up then?
Sure if you want. I was waiting for Wedson to resend the in-place module initialization patch so I could base my stuff on top of it.
Oh how far off is that? Seems like it could conflict with modparams too
I guess it's reasonable to say modparams and this change will go in first.
I guess the 2024 edition isn't entirely deprecating static mut. They're just outlawing everything but addr_of[_mut]!(MY_STATIc_MUT). https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/123758
So we could continue to use static mut as long as we only access it with addr_of[_mut]!.
Yeah, I've been talking to Ralf and coming to the same conclusions. It's a bit weird that addr_of_mut! doesn't work
What do you mean by "addr_of_mut! doesn't work"?
Without const_mut_refs I mean
It seems like addr_of! will soon work without unsafe, which is also good https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/125834
I think I read somewhere that &rawwill be stabilized so the macros are not required.
Ah, yeah, but &raw is just a syntax change.
https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/127679
Aiui that just allows an alternative to addr_of!, addr_of_mut! will still not work in const until const_mut_refs merges. Which is a bit weird
So I think casting the const pointer to mut is fine here
sigh it is not fine. A SyncUnsafeCell is the only way to do what we need to do without UB
Specifically, there is no safe way to create a mutable pointer to a static mut at compile time, until const_mut_refs. Which we don't want to use at this point, it has been changing a lot recently
Coming from https://rust-lang.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/136281-t-opsem/topic/Replacing.20.60static.20mut.60 if anyone wants the whole thread
I don't know about module params, but I don't think we need to avoid static mut for the module itself. We can just use addr_of_mut!. It's not happening in const context, so there's no problem.
Agreed, modparams just requires a a pointer to a static in another static
Which unstable feature is it you need?
We're having something similar stabilized for vtables.
const_mut_refs to use addr_of_mut! in a static
You need const_refs_to_static or something like that right?
I'm sure they are related to some extent
I'm... not convinced this is correct: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/6e6efc5fef4a1cdcccca3cffd5b73fd25d093352/rust/macros/module.rs#L220
Is __this_module a static mut? I would have thought it was a static
Well that's the question, really
I think it has a refcount,?
I think you're right that this is also UB if it gets written through that pointer at any time
Also THIS_MODULE can just be a const.
It probably is a static only to avoid using const_refs_to_static
Do we have the bindings docs hosted anywhere? I'm not on my build machine
Needing an *mut in this API seems unusual in any case
Doesn't look like bindings is visible on rust.docs.kernel.org
Is const_mut_refs planned to be stabilized?
Yes, Ralf is working on it now so I think maybe 2 versions
Then I think we just use it. We need it to fix THIS_MODULE too.

I think we should wait for it to go stable. There have been like 6 PRs in the past two weeks adjusting the rules or checks related to it, the sharp edges are potentially bad so we don't want to run into them
There isn't much reason to not use UnsafeCell imo
We can't do that for __this_module since it's bindgen output
I think existing stuff is fine and doesn't need to change, just for anything added between now and when we get const_mut_refs
I can still send a patch for a wrapper type if it helps, with the intent that it gets used for modparams and maybe a couple other things

Trevor Gross said:

sigh it is not fine. A SyncUnsafeCell is the only way to do what we need to do without UB

Trevor Gross said:

Specifically, there is no safe way to create a mutable pointer to a static mut at compile time, until const_mut_refs. Which we don't want to use at this point, it has been changing a lot recently

Good to know the world is still sane :)

Alice Ryhl said:

Doesn't look like bindings is visible on rust.docs.kernel.org

I think we should change that, I often check which types bindgen uses

cc @Miguel Ojeda

Honestly, after about four days of discussion on this, I am thoroughly convinced that I will never use static mut in my own code again regardless of whether we get const_mut_refs (non-kernel code, to be clear - no bearing when there are people to review things)
I don't mind using SyncUnsafeCell or static mut + addr_of_mut! + const_mut_refs
since the former is already stable, we should probably prefer that though
I can still send a SyncUnsafeCell RFC that doesn't change anything that already exists. I was drafting a new version of the one in std anyway

Benno Lossin said:

Alice Ryhl said:

Doesn't look like bindings is visible on rust.docs.kernel.org

I think we should change that, I often check which types bindgen uses

cc Miguel Ojeda

I will ask for that (and uapi), but please see https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/288089-General/topic/docs.20for.20uapi/near/463180557
yeah it might be pretty big...

For reference:

243M    Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/core
231M    Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/bindings
128M    Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/uapi
73M     Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/src
12M     Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/alloc
4.4M    Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/kernel
80K     Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/macros
16K     Documentation/output/rust/rustdoc/compiler_builtins
I was surprised we don't have a way to build locally, even if the site docs only publish what we currently have. Maybe we could do that at least?

@Trevor Gross could you give a short recap of why addr_of_mut! with const_mut_refs is not OK? It is my understanding that const_mut_refs is about to be stabilized, so it should be fine to use, at least after stabilization.

Trevor Gross said:

I was surprised we don't have a way to build locally, even if the site docs only publish what we currently have. Maybe we could do that at least?

what do you mean we don't have a way to build locally? we can build the docs locally, or do you mean that bindings is missing?

Andreas Hindborg said:

Trevor Gross could you give a short recap of why addr_of_mut! with const_mut_refs is not OK? It is my understanding that const_mut_refs is about to be stabilized, so it should be fine to use, at least after stabilization.

No I think that is fine. I think that the addr_of! and then cast to *mut is not fine

Andreas Hindborg said:

Trevor Gross could you give a short recap of why addr_of_mut! with const_mut_refs is not OK? It is my understanding that const_mut_refs is about to be stabilized, so it should be fine to use, at least after stabilization.

Because the state of current stable, current beta, and current nightly are all pretty different for the feature. In the past month or so, especially in the past week, all kinds of things have changed with the feature - new problems found, changes to what is and isn't checked to be UB, the semantics of how this gets checked, optimizations, etc.

If https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/129195 had merged then it would be more certain, but considering it's still possible for more tweaks to be needed or some problem to found (and hasn't started FCP yet) I think we need to at least wait on that. At least before actually merging something
Maybe this is okay as long as an actual merge to kernel trails that getting merged in Rust, I don't know how soon you plan to resend something

The problem definitely isn't with just getting the address either since that is trivial, it's more that with that feature enabled we could unknowingly walk into something that is broken on at least one version somewhere else
But again, maybe this is tolerable

Alice Ryhl said:

I'm... not convinced this is correct: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/6e6efc5fef4a1cdcccca3cffd5b73fd25d093352/rust/macros/module.rs#L220

You mean we should fix this like: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/Zgsiumknfshjbi9J@boqun-archlinux/
I guess that would work.
Good call on redefining the static.

Trevor Gross said:

If https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/129195 had merged then it would be more certain, but considering it's still possible for more tweaks to be needed or some problem to found (and hasn't started FCP yet) I think we need to at least wait on that. At least before actually merging something

Do you have a rough idea of the time line they are working with? At least for the module_params patches, Luis has made it clear to me that he has a rather big backlog that needs to be cleared before he takes in the module_params patches. I would not expect them to go in until late Q4 or maybe even 25Q1.

Boqun Feng said:

Alice Ryhl said:

I'm... not convinced this is correct: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/6e6efc5fef4a1cdcccca3cffd5b73fd25d093352/rust/macros/module.rs#L220

You mean we should fix this like: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/Zgsiumknfshjbi9J@boqun-archlinux/

Boqun, do you want to submit a patch that fixes this?

Is it possible to have bindgen generate the UnsafeCell instead of a static mut, rather than having this inline (from @Boqun Feng )?

diff --git a/rust/macros/module.rs b/rust/macros/module.rs
index 293beca0a583..8aa4eed6578c 100644
--- a/rust/macros/module.rs
+++ b/rust/macros/module.rs
@@ -219,7 +219,11 @@ mod __module_init {{
                     // freed until the module is unloaded.
                     #[cfg(MODULE)]
                     static THIS_MODULE: kernel::ThisModule = unsafe {{
-                        kernel::ThisModule::from_ptr(&kernel::bindings::__this_module as *const _ as *mut _)
+                        extern \"C\" {{
+                            static __this_module: kernel::types::Opaque<kernel::bindings::module>;
+                        }}
+
+                        kernel::ThisModule::from_ptr(__this_module.get())
                     }};
                     #[cfg(not(MODULE))]
                     static THIS_MODULE: kernel::ThisModule = unsafe {{
I doubt it
If static mut is outlawed in near future, it does not really make sense to generate the code in bindgen?
It's not quite the case that static mut is getting outlawed. But it becomes impossible to access through anything other than addr_of! / addr_of_mut!.
So e.g. &bindings::__this_module will stop compiling because it's accessing it without using those macros.
Sorry if I am repeating questions that was already answered, but addr_of_mut! for a static mut is fine at the moment, just not in const eval where it would require const_mut_refs?
Correct.

Alice Ryhl said:

Boqun Feng said:

Alice Ryhl said:

I'm... not convinced this is correct: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/6e6efc5fef4a1cdcccca3cffd5b73fd25d093352/rust/macros/module.rs#L220

You mean we should fix this like: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/Zgsiumknfshjbi9J@boqun-archlinux/

Boqun, do you want to submit a patch that fixes this?

Yeah, I will.

Andreas Hindborg said:

Trevor Gross said:

If https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/129195 had merged then it would be more certain, but considering it's still possible for more tweaks to be needed or some problem to found (and hasn't started FCP yet) I think we need to at least wait on that. At least before actually merging something

Do you have a rough idea of the time line they are working with? At least for the module_params patches, Luis has made it clear to me that he has a rather big backlog that needs to be cleared before he takes in the module_params patches. I would not expect them to go in until late Q4 or maybe even 25Q1.

It will probably be merged by that time, so I guess that timing could work out. I am a bit surprised it hasn't started FCP already, but the scope has changed a couple times so maybe they're just waiting for things to calm down

Boqun Feng said:

Alice Ryhl said:

Boqun, do you want to submit a patch that fixes this?

Yeah, I will.

Done ;-)
